description: Hollow revolved vase/bottle shape, open at top, centered at origin, with profile defined by straight-line segments revolved 360° around Z axis, then shelled to 2mm wall thickness
components: Revolved solid body from polyline profile
Shell operation (2mm wall, open top face)
notes: Profile polyline vertices in order: (25,0), (25,5), (28,25), (22,45), (10,55), (10,65). Revolve this closed profile (close bottom with segment from (25,0) to (0,0) to (0,65) is NOT needed — revolve the open profile as a shell or revolve closed solid then shell). Recommended implementation: (1) Create a closed 2D wire/face by connecting profile points with a bottom closure segment at Z=0 from (25,0) to (0,0) and an axis segment from (0,0) to (0,65) and closing at (0,65) to (10,65), then revolve to get a solid. (2) Alternatively, revolve the outer profile as a solid revolution with implied axis closure. (3) Shell the resulting solid with 2mm thickness, selecting the top face at Z=65 (neck opening, circle of radius 10mm) as the face to remove/open. The inner cavity will mirror the outer profile offset inward by 2mm. Ensure the bottom remains closed (no hole at base). CadQuery implementation: use cadquery Workplane revolve with a spline or polyline wire, or use shell() after revolve. The top open face is the annular/circular face at Z=65.
